title: What happened to the world in instrumentality
display_title: What happened to the world in instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: What happened to the world in instrumentality?             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 1:18 pm One thing that bothers me in EoE is what happened to the world when Shinji said he wanted everyone to die? I know everyone gets turned to tang, I get that part. Then the Mass Production fused with Rei shove the fake lances in their cores and the world becomes red and crosses start going growing everywhere with a really loud screaming. So what happened there? Is that where everyone else was turned to tang? If so, how come it shows the staff being turned first while everyone else is tanged after the MP Evas do whatever? So after Shinji rejects instrumentality, Yui says everyone can come back. From the little stuff we see. The planet it died red and Shinji is clearly in some sort of wasteland. What did those crosses do exactly? Did they destroy all the buildings therefore destroying all technology like the Magi? I ask because it seems to have some sort of anti-technology message. I understand it has an anti-fantasy message but that doesn't make much sense to take it out on the rest of the world. I mean, sure, the child solder concept is tragic but its something they had to do to fight the angels. Shinji is just plain unlucky really while Asuka and Rei had training for years. They did try to give proper help but Misato was kinda bad at it because she was human(which is the main point really). So in other words, what I'm trying to ask is: Did instrumentality in EoE turn the world into a complete wasteland? Also, no talk about Rebuild being a sequel because we don't know if thats 100% true. Plus I want to know what the original intent was at the time and rebuild didn't get announced and created until a lot later after the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 1:31 pm I try not give much importance to what happens in EoE. The whole movie is very abstract, so I don't care that much. But what happens is that everyone is tanged, and I think that there is nothing destroyed, only in Tokyo-3, because the Black Moon comes out of it. And why does the Nerv personel die earlier? Don't know either. But I like how they die first, and then the real apocalypse begins. And we don't know if the MP EVAs cause everything. Yes, they are shown before the crosses appear, but I think that it's more important that it's Gendo "dying", MP EVAs doing mindfuckery things, and then "AHHHHH!!!" everyone "dies". It doesn't make much sense on a narrative level, but it has a certain rythm, and in this sense it's perfect. That's what I like about this movie. I hope what I wrote is understandable (because of my englitch). Oh, and Rebuild won't be a sequel, if it is it's just for the fun, without being too important, but I think of the signs that "show us that it is a sequel" as things that have to do with the Rebuild story but also act as easter eggs, and as something that is there to confuse us, so that we later say "Ah, so it was that!", but at the same time even in the end making us doubt when we see that first image of Eva 1.11. It's fun!

[]:             Quote Post by MarioAyanami128 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 1:36 pm "Also, no talk about Rebuild being a sequel because we don't know if thats 100% true. Plus I want to know what the original intent was at the time and rebuild didn't get announced and created until a lot later after the movie." - VSimplyMason0 Seriously, we shall never know, even after Final. Anno will certainly troll us. Take my word for granted. :P

[]: Re: What happened to the world in instrumentality?             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Mar 02, 2013 1:38 pm SimplyMason0 wrote: Did instrumentality in EoE turn the world into a complete wasteland?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Mar 02, 2013 1:43 pm Shinji has been looting something to have survived for more than a few days so there are still buildings around and we did see trees when Rei's head was falling. This really comes down to what exactly do you consider a "wasteland".

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sat Mar 02, 2013 3:54 pm From my understanding, what Instrumentality did was return all Lilim, down to the smallest bacteria, to their base form as single self-sustaining collective being, there to have their spirits dissolve and settle down in peace and inertness, which brings death. Shinji and Asuka, however, chose to retain their self-spirits and to live. My opinion is that some other spirits (not neccesarily human ones- bacteria also have some sort of spirit inside them) also chose to reform, and many will reform. I have no idea how long it would take for the Instrumentality Sea to release all spirits and vanish; it could be millions of years. However, since Instrumentality is based on all Lilim, as more leave the chances of more leaving increase, as it becomes increasingly unstable. I would imagine that buildings and technology would remained, provided they weren't physically damaged by the waves of LCL. Evidently only living Lilim have spirits and so degrade, so dead organic matter such as wood also remained. It's been suggested that as LCL is the stuff of life, one might be able to drink it. I think that whatever Lilim ecology and civilization arises from the Sea of Instrumentality, it will be very different from what came before.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 02, 2013 4:01 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: we did see trees when Rei's head was falling.

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 4:08 pm Mr. Tines wrote: That's not all we see

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sat Mar 02, 2013 4:09 pm SimplyMason0 wrote: The crosses blocked the view of the city. So I don't think that stuff was really there.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Mar 02, 2013 5:57 pm Ceci n'est pas un ville

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Mar 02, 2013 10:39 pm I still don't get why Tines makes a big deal about the splash at the same time as saying Shinji has been back for weeks or more. He has to be getting clean water somewhere and hundred mile hikes are hilariously impractical. Given the position of the head I'd wager that city is the place Shinji has been looting so the splash did what exactly?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 03, 2013 12:52 pm The peak wave height hitting the coast of Japan in the 11-Mar-11 tsunami event was 10 meters. That splash is miles high, and would make the event of two years ago look like someone had just let their bath overflow. Of course, in a body forged by power of will and imagination alone, such matters as food and drink become less relevant.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Mar 03, 2013 1:16 pm I believe the more fundamental details of Shinji's survival post-3I to be widely irrelevant. As kuribo-04 stated earlier, EoE is very abstract. We're given what we need to be shown in order to understand the message, not fill in the minor blanks; we see that Shinji is in somewhat of a wasteland that he created, but we don't need to know how exactly he is surviving in such a place. Remember that part of the message of the film is to teach yourself how to live through the hardships of life, but it is not a manual for learning how to do so exactly. Basically, Shinji has to realize that life is hard, painful, and unpredictable; the wasteland setting is appropriate as a symbol for the first day of the rest of his life. Now, as for whether or not the wasteland is planet-wide, that's difficult to determine. We can verify for a fact that 3rd Impact exposed the Black Moon and destroyed much of the surrounding area, so that can easily explain the wasteland setting of One More Final (as well as GNR's giant falling head) in the context of Tokyo-3/Japan. Significant damage may have occurred across the rest of the world due to tsunamis and flooding, and with a lack of people to support the infrastructure (depending on the passage of time between pre- and post-3I), things may have naturally just fallen ill to the elements. Personally, though, I doubt the rest of the world looks as bad as what we see through Shinji's eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Mar 03, 2013 2:02 pm I'm well aware of what should happen Tines but, you know, anime physics aren't exactly absent from the series. Yui talks about regaining your original form so there is no reason to think he doesn't need little things like water which means he has to be looting something . Which in turn means something survived the splash to be looted. There are still buildings standing in the crater and what looks like the silhouette of a tree line on the far side of it. To me everything points to a massive physics derp here. Yui (voice): Don't worry. All living things have the ability to return to their original form...

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Mar 03, 2013 4:10 pm Shinji drinks the LCL. It has all the nutrients that a Lilim could ever need. Problem solved.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Mar 03, 2013 4:50 pm If the head falls offshore there won't be much of a tsunami in Japan anyway; North America will take the brunt of that hit, assuming it's not already fucked for other reasons. Also, GNR's scale is massively open to speculation. At one point she's Eva-sized, at another she's around 120 km or so in height (judging by her size relative to the Black Moon), at another she's continent-sized, and down to 12 km or so toward the end(if Yui's emergence from her eye is any indicator). Unless we're ready to blow off specific scenes to artistic license (at which point you could blow off the whole dang movie, including the scenes used as evidence of widespread devastation around the world) it's probably best to avoid reading too much into them and just run with whatever works for the story at hand.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 03, 2013 6:33 pm Bagheera : Lilith swells up once after Rei 3 returns, and then again near the beginning of "Komm, susser Tod". There are no other implied size changes. Granted, "Jumbo GNR" is not drawn at a perfectly consistent size throughout, but from start to finish, the Black Moon (one of the few things in the NGE universe with a fixed size) is small enough to be cupped between her palms. Objects so small that they would become otherwise invisible compared to GNR -- namely, Evas and the Tree of Life -- are subject to artistic license scaling. This is not just in the "eye scream" shot; this is throughout. Trying to downplay GNR's size in the context of this discussion is futile, IMO. In the final scene, they quite deliberately put her head and arm behind distant mountains and clouds -- as to, well, evoke gargantuan size. (But just because I'm saying this, don't get the idea that I'm supporting Tines or anything.) I'll post some stills if anyone finds it helpful.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Mar 03, 2013 6:43 pm Reichu wrote: Trying to downplay GNR's size in the context of this discussion is futile, IMO. In the final scene, they quite deliberately put her head and arm behind distant mountains and clouds -- as to, well, evoke gargantuan size. (But just because I'm saying this, don't get the idea that I'm supporting Tines or anything.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 03, 2013 7:10 pm Bagheera wrote: Oh Reichu, you're so tsundere!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Mar 03, 2013 7:14 pm Reichu wrote: I'm not quite sure what you mean by that. I was kind of hoping to get my point across this time, but I have no idea if I succeeded.
